hypothesis: Incorporating a Dirichlet-process-based noise schedule into the forward diffusion process—replacing the standard Gaussian noise with Dirichlet-distributed perturbations that inherently respect the simplex geometry of compositional data—will simultaneously eliminate the need for explicit non-negativity and sum-to-one regularization terms while producing generated composite compositions with higher validity and coverage than PCDiff's penalty-based approach, because the intrinsic support of the Dirichlet distribution is the probability simplex, making physical constraint violations structurally impossible rather than merely discouraged.
